close_reason: Implemented in 0253f26
description: API calls have timeout settings but no retry logic. Network blips, rate limiting, and transient model errors cause entire classification runs to fail. Add retry with exponential backoff (e.g., using tenacity or manual implementation) for all HTTP requests in LLMClient. Include configurable max_retries and initial delay. Log each retry attempt with reason.
title: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for API calls

close_reason: Implemented in 2fd5946
description: Currently there is no way to inspect what the LLM is doing during an agentic run. Debugging failures requires guesswork. Add a --debug flag that saves the full conversation history (messages list) to a file (e.g., ~/.git-smart-commit-debug-<timestamp>.json) and optionally prints tool call inputs/outputs to stderr. This makes it possible to diagnose model confusion, bad tool results, and API errors.
title: Add --debug flag to log all API calls and tool results

description: After extracting shared constants, rewrite SYSTEM_PROMPT and AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT as compositions of the shared parts plus their unique content. SYSTEM_PROMPT unique content: read_file tool reference, propose_commits call instruction. AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT unique content: tool descriptions (read_file, get_diff, get_git_log, search_diff, query_tool_result, emit_commit, finalize_commits), context management section, investigation strategy section. Verify the final composed strings read naturally and have no awkward joins or redundant transitions.
title: Compose SYSTEM_PROMPT and AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT from shared parts

description: Add a list_symbols(path) tool that returns a structured list of top-level symbols (functions, classes, methods, constants, exports) with their line numbers and kinds. Helps the agent understand file structure and group related changes without reading entire files. Implementation options discussed in gsc-c3z context — see notes field for details. Should support a wide range of languages since git-smart-commit is language-agnostic.
notes: Implementation: use universal-ctags via subprocess. Shell out to ctags --output-format=json --fields=+nKS -f - <path>. Parse JSON lines, sort by line number, format with indent for scoped symbols. Require ctags installed (nix shell nixpkgs#universal-ctags). No tree-sitter or regex fallback — keep it simple.
title: Add list_symbols tool for agentic mode

close_reason: Implemented in b9bab0d
description: collect_hunks() calls 'git diff' without the --binary flag and does not check for the 'Binary files ... differ' marker. If a binary file is modified, split_hunks() may misparse its diff output, producing invalid patch text that causes 'git apply --cached' to fail. Fix: detect binary diffs (check for 'Binary files' marker or GIT_BINARY_PATCH header), mark them in DiffHunk (e.g. is_binary flag), exclude binary content from the LLM context, and use 'git add' instead of 'git apply --cached' for binary hunks during commit execution.
title: Detect and handle binary files in diff collection

description: After the background compaction thread completes, splice the resulting summary node into the conversation history between LLM turns. The swap must be atomic from the LLM's perspective — it should see a consistent context on each turn, never a partially-swapped state. The summary should include stable IDs so query_tool_result still works for the compacted content.
title: Atomic context swap of compacted summaries between turns

close_reason: Created src/steropes/ with all modules: text.py, config.py, types.py, tools.py, parsers.py, client.py, ui.py, __init__.py, pyproject.toml. Imports verified.

title: Create steropes framework package skeleton

close_reason: Implemented in 9842f26..23d06dd
description: collect_hunks() runs 'git reset HEAD' on staged files (line 1620) to normalize the working tree, but has no try/finally to restore staging if the user interrupts (Ctrl-C) or an exception occurs after the reset but before commits are executed. This silently loses the user's intentional staging. Wrap the collect_hunks() + execute flow in a try/finally that re-stages the originally-staged files on failure. Could save the list from line 1618 and re-run 'git add' on cleanup.
title: Restore staging state on KeyboardInterrupt in collect_hunks()

close_reason: Fixed in bd81f49. Added _strip_breaking_change_trailer() to remove LLM-baked trailers from body; fixed _merge_two_commits() to combine distinct breaking_change fields.
description: Occasionally, committed messages end up with the 'BREAKING CHANGE:' trailer repeated multiple times. Likely causes: (1) the LLM emits a breaking_change field AND includes a trailer in the body text, and both get rendered; (2) the merge path combines breaking_change fields from overlapping commits without deduplication; (3) finalize/formatting code appends the trailer without checking if one already exists in the body. Need to trace the code paths that append breaking-change trailers (likely in commit formatting / git commit invocation) and ensure exactly one trailer appears.
title: Duplicate 'Breaking change' trailers on commit messages

close_reason: Implemented in commit e983f0e — three-level summarization escalation (normal → aggressive bullets → deterministic truncate)
description: Replace single-level _summarize_and_store with a three-level escalation protocol (per LCM paper). Level 1: normal LLM summarize (preserve details, target T tokens). Level 2: aggressive bullet points (target T/2 tokens). Level 3: deterministic truncation (512 tokens, no LLM call). Each level only escalates if the summary is not shorter than the input. Guarantees convergence — eliminates compaction failure where the LLM produces a summary longer than the original. Reference: LCM paper Section 2.3, Figure 3.
title: Add three-level summarization escalation

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified

Leave existing SYSTEM_PROMPT untouched. (2) New build_file_metadata() method on GitAnalyzer that returns compact stats-only summary: git diff --stat for staged/unstaged + untracked file list. No full diffs - the LLM will use get_diff tool lazily.
title: Add AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT and build_file_metadata on GitAnalyzer

close_reason: Implemented in 606e103
description: When executing commits, if git apply fails mid-sequence (after some hunks have already been applied and committed), the working tree is left in a partially-committed state with no rollback. The user gets no clean recovery path. Fix: before executing commits, save the full pre-execution git state (stash or reflog anchor), and on failure, offer to restore it. At minimum, print clear instructions for manual recovery (git reset --soft HEAD~N).
title: Implement rollback on partial git apply failure

close_reason: Multi-turn agentic tool loop fully implemented: agentic_loop, 4 investigation tools (read_file, get_diff, get_git_log, search_diff), AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, classify_changes wired up. Pushed.
description:
## Problem
classify_changes() currently uses call_ollama_with_tool() which forces exactly ONE tool call then exits. The LLM has no ability to investigate before committing to a grouping.

## Solution
Refactor classify_changes() into a multi-turn conversation loop:
1. Send system prompt + changed file list + metadata (not full diffs) to LLM
2. LLM may call investigation tools (read_file, get_diff, get_git_log, search_diff) any number of times
3. Each tool result is appended to the conversation as a tool response message
4. Loop continues until LLM calls propose_commits (terminal/output tool)
5. Add a max_turns safeguard (e.g. 20) to prevent infinite loops

This requires reworking _build_payload() and call_ollama_with_tool() to support multi-message conversation history and multiple available tools simultaneously.

The OpenAI tool_choice field should be set to 'auto' (not forced to a specific tool) during the investigation phase, and only switch to forced propose_commits if max_turns is reached.
title: Implement multi-turn agentic tool loop for classify_changes

description: The codebase uses raw ANSI escape sequences for terminal coloring and formatting. Replace with the rich library for consistent, portable terminal output. Benefits: automatic terminal capability detection, no-color/force-color support, cleaner code, and richer formatting options (tables, progress bars, panels) if needed in the future.
title: Use rich library instead of ad-hoc ANSI escape codes

All methods must handle both Ollama and OpenAI formats. Existing methods stay untouched.
title: Add ToolCall dataclass and LLMClient multi-turn helper methods

description:
Configuration comes from 4 sources (CLI args, env vars, git config, .smart-commit.toml) with a priority order, but there is no way to see the resolved config and no validation of values. Specific gaps:
- No URL format validation on api-base (fails late at httpx.post)
- TOML parse errors indistinguishable from file-not-found (both print generic warning)
- Issue patterns from git config and TOML are merged without clear dedup semantics

Add:
1. A --show-config flag that prints the final resolved configuration and exits
2. URL validation on api-base at startup
3. Distinct error messages for TOML file-not-found vs parse errors
4. A Pydantic model for the TOML schema with validation
title: Add config validation and --show-config flag

close_reason: Implemented in cc71fe8
description: Update AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT to reflect the summary-first context management model. Key changes: (1) Document that tool results are summarized by default — the LLM sees compact summaries, not raw output. (2) Emphasize query_tool_result as the primary way to access details, not a fallback. (3) Instruct the LLM to NOT re-request full file content via read_file after seeing a summary — use query_tool_result instead. (4) Note that summary IDs are stable and can be queried multiple times. This should prevent the context thrashing pattern where the LLM calls read_file repeatedly, each time getting the result summarized. Must coordinate with gsc-59q (incremental emit prompt changes) to avoid conflicts.
title: Update system prompt for summary-first context workflow

description: Replace single CONTEXT_TRIM_THRESHOLD with two thresholds: τ_soft and τ_hard. Below τ_soft: zero overhead, no summarization of already-ingested messages. Between τ_soft and τ_hard: trigger compaction asynchronously between turns (summarize oldest messages while the user/system proceeds). Above τ_hard: block and compact before next LLM call. For Ollama with small context windows, this allows earlier compaction without blocking. The soft threshold enables the zero-cost continuity property — short sessions pay no compaction overhead. Reference: LCM paper Section 2.4, Figure 2.
title: Dual soft/hard compaction thresholds

description:
Currently all LLM calls (reasoning, summarization, query_tool_result) use the same model. Lightweight tasks like summarization and content retrieval don't need the full reasoning model and would benefit from a cheaper/faster auxiliary model (e.g., Haiku).

Calls to route to the auxiliary model:
- query_tool_result: targeted Q&A against stored text
- L1/L2 summarization: compressing tool results
- Convention cache generation (gsc-g9j): analyzing commit history patterns
- Type-aware exploration summaries (gsc-xi7): generating file structure summaries

Calls that must stay on the primary model:
- Commit grouping and emit_commit (core judgment)
- Code issue detection (strong reasoning needed)
- Merge conflict resolution (nuanced decisions)

Implementation: add an auxiliary_model config option (defaulting to the primary model for backward compat). LLMClient gains a method for auxiliary calls, or accepts a model override parameter. This mirrors the LCM paper's approach where Volt uses Haiku alongside Opus for high-throughput subtasks.
title: Add configurable auxiliary model for lightweight LLM calls

close_reason: Closed
description: gsc-zsd needs a clearer description before work can begin.
title: Clarify scope for handle-merges-better (gsc-zsd)

close_reason: Implemented in d8505eb..cc0a69b
description: Hunk IDs use format 'filepath#N' (e.g. src/main.py#1). If a filename contains '#' (e.g. my#file.py), the ID becomes 'my#file.py#1'. Code at lines 2160, 2245, 2866 uses rsplit('#', 1) to extract the filename, which handles this correctly — but the LLM receives these ambiguous IDs and may misparse them when proposing commits. Additionally, any future code that uses split('#') instead of rsplit would silently break. Either URL-encode '#' in filenames within hunk IDs, or use a delimiter that cannot appear in POSIX filenames (e.g. NUL or a multi-char sentinel like '::').
title: Hunk ID delimiter collision when filenames contain '#'

description: The current commit message wrapping logic likely uses simple line-based text wrapping, which can break markdown structure (e.g., splitting mid-list-item, breaking code blocks, mangling indented content). Replace with a proper markdown parser that builds an AST, wraps prose nodes at 80 chars while preserving structural elements (code blocks, lists, headers), then serializes back to text. Candidates: mdformat, mistletoe, or markdown-it-py.
title: Use a proper markdown parser with AST for commit message wrapping

close_reason: Implemented in 433f233
description:
Bug: The LLM frequently over-investigates before emitting commits, especially for straightforward changesets. In the reported case, all 11 hunks were documentation files — the LLM could see this from the initial file list — but it still spent turns on get_git_log, then query_tool_result to re-read the same git log, then deliberated about commit types for pure docs changes.

The AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT investigation strategy section (~L329-341) says 'Investigate before committing' and 'It is fine to delay emit_commit until you have a clear mental model' but gives no guidance on when investigation is sufficient. The LLM treats investigation as a default rather than a means to an end.

Improvements needed:
1. Add heuristics for when to skip investigation: e.g., if all changes are in a single directory, are all the same file type, or the diff is small enough to understand from stats alone
2. Explicitly state: do NOT re-query data you've already received — if get_git_log was summarized, extract what you need from the summary, don't query_tool_result to see it again
3. Add a 'bias to action' instruction: once you can articulate a grouping, emit it — don't seek additional confirmation
4. Clarify that docs-only, test-only, and config-only changesets rarely need deep investigation

This is related to gsc-c3z (prompt consistency) but is a distinct behavioral issue.
title: Tighten investigation strategy guidance to reduce over-deliberation

description: In execute_commits() (line 1976-1980), unknown hunk IDs from the model are silently skipped with a stderr warning. This means a commit can silently lose hunks the LLM intended to include. Validation should happen earlier — after the model proposes commits but before any git state is modified — so that invalid IDs can be reported back to the agentic loop for correction, or the user can be warned in the TUI before confirming execution.
title: Validate proposed hunk IDs against hunk_map before executing commits

close_reason: Implemented in commit e983f0e — _read_file resolves symlinks via Path.resolve() before containment check
description: The _read_file tool checks path.startswith(str(self.repo) + os.sep) to prevent reading outside the repo. However, symlinks inside the repo can resolve to paths outside it, bypassing this check. Fix by resolving the path with path.resolve() before the startswith check, and comparing against self.repo.resolve(). This closes a potential security issue where a malicious diff could cause the model to read /etc/passwd etc.
title: Fix symlink bypass in read_file path traversal protection

description: When the agent summarizes a file (via read_file or diff context), store the summary in ~/.cache/gsc/<repo-hash>/summaries/<blob-sha>.txt keyed by the file's git blob SHA. On subsequent runs, if the blob SHA matches, reuse the cached summary instead of making an LLM call. Saves significant time/cost on large repos where the same unchanged files provide context across runs. Include a TTL or max-cache-size eviction policy.
title: Cache file-level summaries in ~/.cache/gsc/

description:
Patches in DiffHunk.patch are derived from 'git diff' when the index is at HEAD (after collect_hunks() resets staged files). During execute_commits(), hunks are applied in commit order via 'git apply --cached'. If file X has hunks assigned to commits 1 and 2, applying commit 1's hunks shifts line numbers in the index. Commit 2's patch for file X still has the original (HEAD-relative) @@ line numbers and context, so 'git apply --cached' may fail to locate the correct position — especially for adjacent or nearby hunks.

## Patch theory framing

In patch theory (as implemented by Darcs and Pijul), two patches on the same file are *independent* (they *commute*) if their affected line ranges do not overlap and are not adjacent — i.e., reordering them produces the same result. Patches that are not independent have a dependency: the second can only be correctly applied after the first.

For this tool, hunks from the same file that are assigned to different commits should be checked for independence before execution:
- If all intra-file hunks across commits are pairwise independent (non-overlapping, non-adjacent regions), application order does not matter and git apply --cached will succeed regardless of commit order.
- If any two intra-file hunks are dependent (overlapping or adjacent regions), the LLM has proposed a split that cannot be cleanly applied — either warn the user, merge those commits, or error out.

## Potential fixes

1. **Detect non-independent splits at proposal time**: after LLM proposes commits, scan hunk_map for files whose hunks span multiple commits; compute line-range overlap; reject or warn on dependent splits before touching the index.
2. **Re-derive patches after each commit**: after committing, re-run 'git diff' to get fresh patches for remaining hunks. Correct but expensive (one diff per file per remaining commit).
3. **Use 'git apply --3way'** as a fallback when exact context match fails, falling back to merge-conflict resolution.
4. **Sort hunks within a file in line-order** before applying, so earlier hunks don't invalidate later ones' context — partial mitigation only.
title: Hunk context mismatch when same file spans multiple commits

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified
description:
## Purpose
Allow the LLM to read the full current content of any changed file, not just its diff.

## Why Needed
Diffs show what changed but not the surrounding context. For example:
- Which class/module does a changed function belong to?
- What imports already exist?
- What does the rest of the file look like?

## Implementation
- Add a read_file(path: str) -> str tool in the @tool registry
- Path must be validated against the repo root (no path traversal)
- Should only allow reading files that are in the changed files list OR exist in the repo
- Truncate at a reasonable limit (e.g. 50KB) with a note if truncated
- Depends on: multi-turn agentic loop (gsc-*) being implemented first
title: Add read_file tool for LLM to fetch full file contents

description:
git-smart-commit needs to be able to playback a plan from a previous sessio


Currently, `git smart-commit --json` return a JSON plan for commits
but there is no way to apply that plan exactly, making the "plan then
execute" workflow unpredictable. Implement an "execute plan" workflow
in `git-smart-commit` that recreates a plan from a JSON description
and then runs it.  Also, update the skill to be aware of this
workflow.
title: git-smart-commit

description: The codebase has multiple system prompts (SYSTEM_PROMPT ~L200, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT ~L297, MERGE_PROMPT ~L1794) that likely have overlapping instructions and inconsistent tone/style. Review all prompts, harmonize their language and conventions, and factor out shared content using string composition (e.g., shared preamble variables, f-string interpolation) — without moving prompts to separate files. Goals: (1) consistent terminology and instruction style across prompts, (2) eliminate duplicated guidance, (3) keep all prompts in git-smart-commit as top-level constants.
title: Tune system prompts for consistency and reduce duplication

description: There are zero automated tests for the critical logic: split_hunks(), merge_overlapping_commits(), _read_file() path validation, hunk ID generation, and git apply sequencing. A pytest suite with fixtures (temp git repos, sample diffs) would catch regressions when logic is modified. Start with the pure functions that have no LLM dependency.

close_reason: Implemented in 23fbc5b
description: Shift from reactive to proactive summarization (LCM-style). Currently tool results are appended raw and only summarized when CONTEXT_TRIM_THRESHOLD is exceeded. Change to: every tool result above TOOL_RESULT_SUMMARIZE_SKIP gets summarized immediately on ingest via _summarize_and_store, with the original stored in result_store for query_tool_result access. The LLM always works from compact summaries and drills down deliberately via query_tool_result. This prevents the context thrashing pattern visible in the out log where the LLM reads a 50K file, it gets reactively summarized, then re-requests it, which also gets summarized. Reference: LCM paper Section 2.1 (Active Context vs Immutable Store).
title: Summaries-first tool result handling

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified
description:
## Purpose
Allow the LLM to request the raw diff for a specific file on demand, rather than having all diffs pre-loaded into the initial prompt.

## Why Needed
Currently ALL diffs are pre-fetched and sent in the initial prompt, which:
- Creates a huge initial prompt (up to DIFF_TOTAL_CHARS = 120k chars)
- Requires lossy hunk summarization for large files
- Sends irrelevant diffs the LLM may not need

With lazy fetching, the initial prompt shrinks to just file list + stats.
The LLM requests only the diffs it actually needs to decide groupings.

## Implementation
- Add get_diff(path: str, staged: bool | None = None) -> str tool
- staged=None means show both staged and unstaged (combined)
- staged=True means --cached, staged=False means unstaged only
- No summarization needed since the LLM is requesting exactly what it wants
- Should respect DIFF_CHARS_PER_FILE limit with a truncation note
- Depends on: multi-turn agentic loop
title: Add get_diff tool for lazy per-file diff fetching

close_reason: Already implemented: git apply --cached is already used at execute_commits line 1995.
description: When executing commits, the current code uses plain 'git apply' which modifies both the index and the working tree. This is wrong: the hunks being committed are already present in the worktree (they're the user's own changes). Applying them again to the worktree is at best a no-op and at worst corrupts unstaged changes. The correct approach is 'git apply --cached' (apply only to the index, leaving the worktree untouched), followed by 'git commit'. This is analogous to 'git add -p' behavior and is the safe, correct way to selectively stage hunks.
title: Use 'git apply --cached' to avoid disturbing the worktree during commit execution

close_reason: Token/cost reporting implemented. TokenUsage dataclass accumulates prompt+completion tokens across agentic loop, call_with_tool, and streaming call. Summary printed to stderr after classify_changes.
description: After classification, print token usage (prompt/completion tokens) and estimated cost. Extract usage field from API responses. Support per-call and total accumulation across the agentic loop. Show summary at end of run.
title: Token/cost reporting

description: MERGE_PROMPT (L1794-1813) currently has its own condensed versions of type-priority rules ('feat type must take priority', 'chore should only be used if no other category applies') and breaking-change rules. After the shared constants exist, update MERGE_PROMPT to reference them for consistency, while keeping its merge-specific instructions (combine into single commit, dedup hunks/issues, write coherent subject). Ensure the merge-specific guidance doesn't contradict the shared rules.
title: Harmonize MERGE_PROMPT with shared constants

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified
description: Core agentic loop method on LLMClient. Takes system_prompt, initial_user_message, tool_registry (investigation tools dict), terminal_tool (propose_commits), and kwargs for both. Builds [system, user] initial messages. Each turn: send messages -> extract tool call -> dispatch. Investigation tool results appended to history. Terminal tool validated with pydantic and returned. Validation errors for terminal tool become tool result messages. No tool call -> nudge with user message. Last turn -> restrict tool list to terminal tool only. Progress logged to stderr. MAX_AGENTIC_TURNS=20 constant. Depends on ToolCall dataclass and helper methods.
title: Add agentic_loop method to LLMClient

close_reason: Implemented hunk-level diff processing and partial staging via git apply --cached. DiffHunk dataclass, collect_hunks(), build_hunk_context(), updated Commit model (files→hunks), execute_commits uses git apply --cached, merge detection now hunk-aware.
description: Refactor the diff processing pipeline to handle individual hunks end-to-end rather than whole files. This enables staging partial file changes using 'git apply' on hunk-level patches, giving finer-grained control over what goes into each commit. The full pipeline—analysis, lint suggestions, commit message generation—should operate on a per-hunk basis.
title: Thread diff hunks individually to enable partial file staging via git apply

close_reason: Implemented delete-commit (d), re-classify-with-critique (r), _CritiqueModal, _Reclassify sentinel, _list_gen DuplicateIds fix, text-mode d/r commands, and main() reclassify loop.
description:
The TUI currently lets users review proposed commits and edit individual commit subjects before confirming. This issue is about giving users richer control over the plan itself:

**Plan editing tools:**
- Move hunks between commits: select a file/hunk in the detail panel and reassign it to a different commit
- Split a commit: divide one proposed commit into two, choosing which hunks go where
- Merge commits: user-initiated merge of two commits into one
- Delete a commit: drop a proposed commit entirely (its hunks stay unstaged)
- Edit commit body: allow editing the full body text, not just the subject
- Add a new empty commit slot and assign hunks to it

**Re-classification tools:**
- 'r' keybinding to re-run classification with a critique: show an Input field where the user can type feedback (e.g. 'put the test changes in a separate commit'), then re-invoke classify_changes() with that critique and refresh the TUI with the new plan
- 'R' or a separate action to submit arbitrary edits to the current plan as a diff/patch and get a new plan back — i.e., user edits the JSON plan inline and triggers re-classification

The goal is to make grouping mistakes fully recoverable in the TUI without leaving the tool, and to support iterative refinement of the commit plan.
title: UI tools for modifying the commit plan

close_reason: Closed
description: gsc-zsd needs a clearer description before work can begin. What specific merge scenarios should be improved? e.g. mid-conflict state detection, worktree divergence handling, skill merge-back workflow.
title: Clarify scope for handle-merges-better (gsc-zsd)

description: Epic: Implement asynchronous context compaction based on the LCM paper's dual-threshold architecture. Currently, summarization is synchronous and inline — it blocks the agentic loop while compacting. The LCM approach uses two thresholds: τ_soft triggers async compaction in the background while the LLM keeps working, and τ_hard blocks only when context is critically full. This eliminates user-facing latency for most compaction events while guaranteeing the context never overflows.
title: Async context compaction (LCM-inspired)

description: Analyze the repo's recent commit history (types, scopes, body style, subject length patterns) and cache the result in ~/.cache/gsc/<repo-hash>/conventions.json. Keyed by HEAD sha — invalidate when new commits appear. The cached conventions get injected into the system prompt, eliminating the get_git_log tool call that happens on almost every run. Store: most-used types, scope vocabulary, average body length, any project-specific patterns.
title: Cache project commit conventions in ~/.cache/gsc/

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified
description:
## Purpose
Allow the LLM to inspect the repo's recent commit history to learn the project's commit conventions.

## Why Needed
Different repos have different commit styles:
- Some use scopes consistently (feat(auth):), some never do
- Some have specific type vocabularies
- Some are monorepos with directory-based scopes
- Some add ticket numbers, emojis, etc.

Without this, the LLM applies generic conventions that may not match the project.

## Implementation
- Add get_git_log(n: int = 20) -> str tool
- Returns the last n commit subjects (one-line format: hash + subject)
- Optionally also show --stat to show which files each commit touched
- Cap n at 50 to avoid flooding context
- Depends on: multi-turn agentic loop
title: Add get_git_log tool for repo commit style awareness

description: Use a good hash function to identify hunks rather than the current pattern of <filename>#<number>.  Consider whether the properties of a gear hash are useful or if we should use something more standard like truncated SHA
title: Identify hunks by their hashes not by filename#number

This inventory drives all subsequent refactoring. Key known duplications: opening sentence, junk-file paragraph, code-issue examples (~40 lines of Java/C/Python), hunk-ID rules, cohesive-commit rules, conventional-commit format, type definitions, breaking-change detection.
notes: Audit complete. See context/prompt-audit.md for full inventory. Key findings: (1) Opening sentence identical in SYSTEM_PROMPT+AGENTIC. (2) Junk-file paragraph identical. (3) ~40-line code-issue examples block verbatim identical. (4) Entire Rules section (15 bullets) verbatim identical. (5) MERGE_PROMPT has semantically equivalent type-priority rule but different wording. Recommended extraction order: CODE_ISSUE_EXAMPLES -> SHARED_RULES (commit format+types+hunk-grouping) -> SHARED_BREAKING_CHANGE+SHARED_ISSUES_FIELD -> MERGE_PROMPT harmonization.

Useful when the agent needs to find usages, definitions, or patterns beyond what's visible in the diff. Should support: pattern (required), glob/file-type filter (optional), context lines (optional), max results cap to avoid huge outputs. Wire into the agentic tool dispatch alongside read_file, get_diff, etc.

close_reason: Implemented in a49eeec
description: Lockfiles (package-lock.json, poetry.lock, Cargo.lock, yarn.lock, uv.lock, etc.) and other machine-generated large files trigger the context summarization path, wasting LLM calls on content that isn't useful to analyze in detail. Add a skip-list of well-known patterns so these files either: (1) get a fixed short summary without an LLM call (e.g., 'lockfile updated, N packages changed'), or (2) are excluded from summarization entirely and represented by their stat line only. The skip-list should be easily extensible.
title: Bypass summarization for well-known large files

description:
When the LLM assigns hunks from the same file to different commits, we need to verify the hunks are independent (commute) before executing. This is the first concrete step from gsc-b7n (patch theory analysis).

Implementation: after LLM proposes commits, scan for files whose hunks span multiple commits. For each such file, parse the @@ line ranges from the hunk patches and check for overlap or adjacency. If any two hunks are dependent (overlapping or adjacent line ranges), either:
- Warn the user in the TUI and suggest merging the affected commits
- Re-prompt the LLM to assign all dependent hunks to the same commit
- Automatically merge the affected commits

This prevents the cross-commit context mismatch bug from ever reaching 'git apply --cached'.
title: Implement hunk independence check before commit execution

close_reason: Implemented in single batch: ToolCall dataclass, LLMClient helpers, agentic_loop, AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, build_file_metadata, all 4 investigation tools, classify_changes wired to agentic loop, main() simplified
description:
## Purpose
Allow the LLM to search for a string or regex pattern across ALL changed file diffs, without having to read each diff individually.

## Why Needed
Some classification decisions depend on finding patterns across the whole changeset:
- Are there TODO/FIXME comments in the changes?
- Which files import a particular module?
- Which files contain test assertions vs production code?
- Are there debug print statements left in?

## Implementation  
- Add search_diff(pattern: str, context_lines: int = 2) -> list[dict] tool
- Runs the pattern against all staged+unstaged diffs
- Returns list of {file, line_number, match_context} dicts
- Limit results to first 50 matches to prevent context flooding
- pattern is a Python regex
- Depends on: multi-turn agentic loop
title: Add search_diff tool for pattern-based change investigation

close_reason: Implemented in 515654c
description: Currently a new httpx.Client is created on every API call (in call(), call_with_tool(), agentic_loop()), causing unnecessary TCP connection overhead. With multi-turn agentic loops making dozens of API calls, this significantly increases latency. Fix: create a single httpx.Client instance at LLMClient initialization and reuse it across all API calls.
title: Add HTTP connection pooling (reuse httpx.Client)

close_reason: Removed --yes from argument-hint; added explicit passthrough/control-flow flag split at top of skill; --yes and --dry-run now handled in prose, not injected into --json commands.
description:
When the skill is invoked with --yes or --dry-run, $ARGUMENTS is substituted literally into all command examples in the skill body, producing nonsense like 'git-smart-commit --json --save-plan /tmp/gsc-plan.json --yes' for the read-only plan step, and 'If --dry-run was in --yes, stop here' in the conditional text.

Root cause: $ARGUMENTS is a flat string substituted everywhere in the skill markdown, so control-flow flags meant only for the execute step bleed into the plan/critique steps and the prose conditionals.

Fix options:
1. Split passthrough flags (--model, --repo, --api-base, --api-key, --critique) from control-flow flags (--yes, --dry-run) in the skill. Extract --yes/--dry-run from $ARGUMENTS and handle them explicitly in the workflow steps rather than passing them through blindly.
2. Remove --yes from argument-hint and have the skill always ask for confirmation explicitly, ignoring any --yes in $ARGUMENTS.
title: skill: --yes and --dry-run in $ARGUMENTS corrupt skill step descriptions

close_reason: Implemented in 69856f2
description:
Currently, plan data (commits, result_store, context) is only persisted to the temp directory on successful completion. If the user hits Ctrl-C or an unhandled exception occurs mid-run, all the LLM's work is lost — the emitted commits, investigation results, and partial plans vanish.

Since agentic runs can be expensive (30-50k+ tokens), losing that work is painful. The plan persistence code already exists (writes to gsc-plan-* temp dirs); it just needs to run on abnormal exit too.

Fix: Add signal handler for SIGINT and wrap the agentic loop in try/finally (or atexit) to call the plan persistence logic. Should persist whatever state exists at interruption time — partial commits, result_store, conversation history. The temp directory path should still be printed to stderr so the user can find it.
title: Persist plan data on keyboard interrupt and unhandled exceptions

close_reason: Implemented Ollama-aware context compression: lower threshold (20K chars), initial diff context now compressible, most-recent tool result protected
description: When commits have large diffs, the full diff content is passed to the LLM upfront, consuming significant context window space. With Ollama (local LLM backend), this causes request timeouts because the model takes too long to process large inputs. Large diffs from generated files, minified code, large refactors, or binary-as-text files are the primary triggers. Mitigation strategies to consider: truncate diffs beyond a configurable size limit, filter out generated/vendored/binary files before sending to the LLM, summarize large hunks, or chunk the diff and analyze incrementally.
title: Large diffs cause Ollama request timeouts

close_reason: Closed
title: Change default model to gpt-oss:20b

close_reason: Implemented in 666dc60
description:
Bug: The LLM sometimes calls finalize_commits after emitting only a small subset of hunks, abandoning the rest silently. In the reported case, 11 hunks across 3 files were analyzed, the LLM emitted a single commit covering 2 hunks, then immediately called finalize_commits — dropping 9 hunks without explanation. The LLM had even narrated planning 7 groupings but bailed after the first.

It's valid to skip hunks (junk files, build artifacts, things the user shouldn't commit). The problem is the LLM calling finalize_commits prematurely without having made a deliberate decision about each remaining hunk.

Fix: When finalize_commits is called with unassigned hunks remaining, require the LLM to demonstrate it has considered them. Options:
1. Add a required 'skipped_hunks' parameter to finalize_commits where the LLM must explicitly list hunks it's intentionally not committing, with brief reasons — reject if unassigned hunks aren't accounted for
2. When unassigned hunks remain, return them in the tool response and ask the LLM to either emit more commits or call finalize_commits again with an explicit acknowledgment
3. Inject a confirmation turn: 'You have N unassigned hunks: [list]. Call finalize_commits again to confirm you are done, or emit more commits.'

The key insight: not every hunk needs a commit, but every hunk needs a decision. The LLM should not be able to silently abandon work.
title: Validate finalize_commits covers all hunks before accepting

description: For read_file results above a size threshold, generate structural summaries based on file type instead of generic LLM summarization. Code files: extract function/class signatures, import list, and high-level structure. Structured data (JSON, CSV): extract schema and shape. Plain text: standard LLM summary. This gives the LLM enough structural awareness to reason about file contents without loading them fully. The LLM can still drill into specifics via query_tool_result. Reference: LCM paper Section 2.2 (Large File Handling, Exploration Summary).
title: Type-aware exploration summaries for read_file

close_reason: Added --quiet/-q flag, _log() helper, _QUIET global, moved all progress output from stderr to stdout with ANSI formatting
description: Basic tool-call logging already exists (prints to stderr: '[agentic turn N/20] tool_name(args)'). This issue is about improving it: move output to stdout so it's visible in normal use, improve formatting for readability, and add a --quiet flag to suppress for scripting/CI use.
title: Show agentic loop tool calls in real time

close_reason: Implemented in 438dc34 — _format_patch_markup renders unified diffs as colour-coded Rich markup in the TUI detail pane
description: Show diffs in UI when viewing a proposed commit
title: Show diffs in UI when viewing proposed commits

close_reason: Implemented in 54ea5bd
description:
Bug: After successfully emitting one commit, the LLM enters a loop where it narrates its intent ('I need to emit the remaining two commits') but never actually makes a tool call. The nudging mechanism fires repeatedly (11+ times in the reported case), each time getting the same non-actionable response. The LLM burns through its entire turn budget producing identical text.

This is likely the LLM generating a response without a tool call, then the nudge mechanism saying 'you didn't call a tool, please continue' — but whatever caused the LLM to omit the tool call on turn 3 keeps recurring on every subsequent turn.

Possible causes:
1. The emit_commit response on turn 2 may have been summarized or truncated in a way that lost the remaining hunk IDs, so the LLM can't construct the next tool call
2. The nudge message may not be effective — it should include actionable context (remaining hunks, what tools are available) rather than just a generic 'continue'
3. The LLM may be hitting a structured output / tool-call generation issue with the specific model

Possible fixes:
1. After N consecutive nudges (e.g., 3) with no tool call, inject the remaining unassigned hunk list explicitly into the conversation to give the LLM concrete data to act on
2. Escalate the nudge: first nudge is gentle, second includes remaining hunks, third forces finalize_commits with what's been emitted so far
3. Detect the 'stuck loop' pattern (identical assistant messages) and break out early rather than burning all remaining turns
4. Ensure emit_commit responses always include the full remaining hunk list in a non-summarizable format
title: LLM gets stuck in nudge loop without making tool calls

close_reason: Implemented read_git_config(); priority chain is CLI arg > env var > git config > default. Keys: smart-commit.model, smart-commit.api-base, smart-commit.api-key.
description:
Currently, `git-smart-commit` assumes that you want to use a
particular Qwen model served by Ollama at a particular URL. This is a
bad assumption and, as a git sub-command, git-smart-commit should be
able to be configured through standard git configuration
options. Research whether we can use `git config` to access our own
section of ~/.config/git/config and, if this is possible, implement
this as a fallback for the various configuration parametes.
title: git-smart-commit should get its configuration from `git config` in addition to command line arguments. (especially for the Ollama URL and related parameters)

description: The conventional commit format block (type(scope): desc, body rules, type definitions for feat/fix/refactor/chore/etc., type priority, no-repeat-type rule, body-from-diff-only rule) is duplicated verbatim in SYSTEM_PROMPT and AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT, and partially restated in MERGE_PROMPT. Extract into a COMMIT_FORMAT_RULES constant and interpolate into all three prompts. MERGE_PROMPT should reference the same definitions rather than having its own condensed version.
title: Extract shared conventional-commit format rules into a constant
